title: Nurse Migration
content_html:
Nurse Migration

There are more nurses today than at any time in history, yet a global nursing shortage threatens to cripple health care systems worldwide. Because the underlying causes are complex and vary in different regions, a simple, short-term fix will not remedy the situation.

In developing nations—particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, and Latin America—a major reason for the shortage is nurse migration to developed countries in Europe and North America as well as Australia to help fill vacancies there.

Nurse migration is hardly a recent phenomenon. The Philippines have for years trained many more nurses than the country needs, with thousands working abroad and sending money to relatives back home (nearing $1 billion annually). From that standpoint, migration of nurses from poorer to wealthier countries would appear to benefit all involved. But there is another side to the story. Today, even the Philippines feels the effects of the nursing shortage, with nurses continuing to migrate abroad while positions at home go unfilled.

Lured by the higher salaries and better quality of life available in wealthier countries, nurses from developing countries frequently leave behind already overburdened health care systems, where nurses are often the primary caregivers because doctors, too, are in short supply. Conditions then deteriorate further as the nurse-to-population ratio declines, a number that is estimated to be ten times higher in European than in African countries. Some Latin American countries are experiencing such a shortage of nurses that doctors actually outnumber them, leaving the bulk of health care up to assistant nurses, who have only rudimentary training.

Routine immunizations and prenatal care fall victim to the nursing shortage in developing countries, and in many cases the results can be life-threatening. In African countries where the HIV/AIDS epidemic is rampant, some patients go untreated not because lifesaving drugs are unavailable but because there are not enough nurses to administer them.

In developed countries in Europe and North America, the nursing shortage largely stems from an aging population, who require more health care services, coupled with a dwindling supply of nurses, many of whom are likewise nearing retirement age, with fewer young people preparing to replace them.

A common thread among nurse-importing countries—underinvestment in nursing education dating back two or more decades—has prevented them from creating a stable workforce to meet current and future needs. The United Kingdom, for example, still feels the effects of a cutback in nurse training some twenty years ago. In the United States, nursing schools turn down thousands of qualified applicants every year because of their own shortages of nursing faculty. Developed countries need to invest in nursing education and focus on retaining and rewarding nurses appropriately, both financially and through high-quality working conditions.

Widespread nurse migration helps neither the host country nor the country of origin in the long run, does nothing to remedy the underlying cause of the shortage, and results in millions of people being deprived of the health care they need.

questions.text: The ______ of nurses in developing nations...
questions.question: The ______ of nurses in developing nations...
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–6

Complete the summary using the list of phrases, A–I, below.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
nurses
developing nations
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 1:

"a global nursing shortage"

Đoạn 2:

"a major reason for the shortage"

Bước 3: Phân tích

Chỗ trống đứng sau "The" và trước "of nurses".

=> cần danh từ.

Từ phù hợp:

shortage of nurses

là cụm xuất hiện xuyên suốt bài.

Đáp án: shortage

_blockConfig.noteContent: The [1] of nurses in developing nations is largely caused by nurses leaving their countries to work [2] It is difficult for poorer nations to retain their nurses because better salaries and living conditions [3] many nurses to work in wealthier countries. When nurses migrate to other countries, there are fewer [4] health care givers left in their own countries. The lack of trained doctors as well as nurses means that health care is often [5] by workers who have only [6] skills.

questions.questionHtml: The 1_______ of nurses in developing nations is largely caused by nurses leaving their countries to work 2_______ It is difficult for poorer nations to retain their nurses because better salaries and living conditions 3_______ many nurses to work in wealthier countries. When nurses migrate to other countries, there are fewer 4_______ health care givers left in their own countries. The lack of trained doctors as well as nurses means that health care is often 5_______ by workers who have only 6_______ skills.

questions.question: to work ______
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
work
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 3:

"thousands working abroad"

Bước 3: Phân tích

work abroad= làm việc ở nước ngoài

Đáp án: abroad

questions.text: better salaries and living conditions ______ many nurses
questions.question: better salaries and living conditions ______ many nurses
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
salaries
living conditions
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 4:

"Lured by the higher salaries and better quality of life"

Bước 3: Phân tích
lured by = bị thu hút bởi
Trong word list có:
lure

Sau chủ ngữ số nhiều:

salaries and conditions lure nurses

Đáp án: lure

questions.text: there are fewer ______ health care givers left
options: Life-threatening conditions due to lack of treatment
questions.question: there are fewer ______ health care givers left
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
health care givers
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 4:

"nurses are often the primary caregivers"

Bước 3: Phân tích

caregivers

=
health care givers

primary được paraphrase thành qualified trong danh sách từ.

Đáp án: qualified

questions.text: health care is often ______ by workers
questions.question: health care is often ______ by workers
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
by workers
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 5:

"there are not enough nurses to administer them"

Bước 3: Phân tích
Cấu trúc bị động:
health care is administered by workers
Đáp án: administered

questions.question: workers who have only ______ skills
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
skills
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 4:

"assistant nurses, who have only rudimentary training"

Bước 3: Phân tích
rudimentary training=rudimentary skills
Đáp án: rudimentary

questions.text: A primary reason for the nursing shortage in developed countries is the health care needs of the aging population.
questions.question: A primary reason for the nursing shortage in developed countries is the health care needs of the aging population.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 7–9

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 7–9 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
developed countries
aging population
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 6:

"the nursing shortage largely stems from an aging population"

Bước 3: Phân tích
largely stems from
=
primary reason

Thông tin hoàn toàn khớp.

Đáp án: TRUE

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.text: There is not a large enough supply of qualified applicants for nursing schools in the United States.
questions.question: There is not a large enough supply of qualified applicants for nursing schools in the United States.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
qualified applicants
United States
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 7:

"nursing schools turn down thousands of qualified applicants"

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc nói:

có hàng ngàn ứng viên đủ điều kiện

Đề nói:

không đủ ứng viên đủ điều kiện

Hai ý ngược nhau.

Đáp án: FALSE

questions.text: In the United Kingdom, a high percentage of nurses have retired during the past two decades.
options: Deprivation of healthcare for millions of people
questions.question: In the United Kingdom, a high percentage of nurses have retired during the past two decades.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
United Kingdom
retired
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 7:

"The United Kingdom ... still feels the effects of a cutback in nurse training"

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài chỉ nói:

cắt giảm đào tạo y tá

Không nói:

bao nhiêu y tá nghỉ hưu

Không có thông tin.

Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.text: Nurses are in .......... supply in many parts of the world.
questions.question: Nurses are in .......... supply in many parts of the world.
questions.explanation:
Câu gốc: Nurses are in short supply in many parts of the world.

Phân tích ngữ pháp: Cụm từ "in ... supply" có "supply" đóng vai trò là Danh từ (nguồn cung). Để bổ nghĩa cho danh từ này, ta bắt buộc phải dùng một Tính từ đứng trước nó. Hơn nữa, "in short supply" là một cụm từ cố định (collocation) trong tiếng Anh mang nghĩa là "tình trạng khan hiếm, thiếu hụt".

Loại trừ đáp án sai:

shortage (Danh từ): Sự thiếu hụt. (Không thể ghép "in shortage supply").

shorten (Động từ): Rút ngắn lại. (Không đứng trước danh từ để bổ nghĩa).

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.text: There are many reasons for this lack of nurses; the issue is filled with ...........
questions.question: There are many reasons for this lack of nurses; the issue is filled with ...........
questions.explanation:
Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống đứng ngay sau giới từ with (trong cụm filled with - chứa đầy). Theo quy tắc ngữ pháp, sau giới từ ta phải dùng một Danh từ hoặc Danh động từ (V-ing). Trong ngữ cảnh này, "vấn đề chứa đầy những điều phức tạp", ta cần một danh từ số nhiều.

Loại trừ đáp án sai:

complex (Tính từ): Phức tạp. (Không đứng độc lập sau giới từ "with" trong trường hợp này).

complexly (Trạng từ): Một cách phức tạp.

questions.text: A solution needs to be found soon, because the problem has become ...........
questions.question: A solution needs to be found soon, because the problem has become ...........
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Tìm từ khóa và xác định thành phần ngữ pháp

Từ khóa là has become.

Bước 2: Định vị vị trí cần điền

Chỗ trống đứng sau động từ nối (linking verb) "become". Sau "become" thường cần:

tính từ (adj), hoặc
danh từ chỉ trạng thái (ít gặp hơn trong trường hợp này).

Ta cần một từ mô tả tình trạng của the problem.

Bước 3: Phân tích từ loại
rampancy (danh từ): sự lan tràn, tình trạng phổ biến rộng khắp.
rampant (tính từ): lan tràn, nghiêm trọng, phổ biến rộng khắp.
rampantly (trạng từ): một cách lan tràn.

Cụm tự nhiên trong tiếng Anh là:

The problem has become rampant.

(= vấn đề đã trở nên nghiêm trọng/lan rộng.)

Nếu dùng rampancy, câu sẽ là:

The problem has become rampancy.

Sai vì rampancy là danh từ, không dùng tự nhiên sau become theo cách này.

Đáp án: rampant

questions.text: When nurses .......... their positions at hospitals and health care centers, it is not easy to find other nurses to replace them.
questions.question: When nurses .......... their positions at hospitals and health care centers, it is not easy to find other nurses to replace them.
questions.explanation:
Câu gốc: When nurses vacate their positions at hospitals...

Phân tích ngữ pháp: Phân tích cấu trúc mệnh đề: When (Từ nối) + nurses (Chủ ngữ số nhiều) + .......... + their positions (Tân ngữ). Vị trí còn thiếu duy nhất để tạo thành một câu hoàn chỉnh chính là một Động từ chính. Vì chủ ngữ "nurses" là số nhiều, động từ chia ở dạng nguyên mẫu.

Loại trừ đáp án sai:

vacancies (Danh từ số nhiều): Những vị trí trống, chỗ trống.

vacant (Tính từ): Trống, bỏ không. (Chưa có động từ thì câu vô nghĩa).

questions.text: The ability to administer health care where it is needed is crippled when there are not enough .......... nurses.
questions.question: The ability to administer health care where it is needed is crippled when there are not enough .......... nurses.
questions.explanation:
Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống đứng giữa đại từ chỉ lượng enough (đủ) và danh từ nurses (những y tá). Để bổ nghĩa cho danh từ "nurses" (những y tá này là người như thế nào?), ta cần một Tính từ. "Qualified" (đủ điều kiện, đủ trình độ chuyên môn) là tính từ hoàn toàn phù hợp về nghĩa.

Loại trừ đáp án sai:

qualifications (Danh từ số nhiều): Các loại bằng cấp, chứng chỉ.

qualifies (Động từ thêm -s): Đủ tiêu chuẩn (chia theo ngôi thứ 3 số ít).
